← In Search of the Perfect 6-Player Risk Board · the 20-map analysis →

risk-lab · viable starter maps

10 buildable boards (degree ≤5, ≤4 distinct countries per territory) shown as physical boards — the top 3 with several layout permutations. Every board beats classic World Risk under the realistic (opening-robust) objective at 500 games. Open in a browser to visualise; each board's JSON imports straight into the hex map editor.

Sanity check — are these Risk-like?

TerritoriesCountriesMax degree
These maps39–498–9≤5 (capped 5)
World Risk4266
Classic Risk4266

Verdict: firmly Risk-like. Territory counts (39–49) bracket Risk's 42; country counts (8–9) run a little above Risk's 6 (by design — it's the fairest band for six players); and every territory is attackable from at most 5 others, tighter than Risk's max of 6. No board is structurally far from the base game.

The 10 maps

#MapctCompositeProgSnowComebackRdsPersona-fairmaxDeg/Ctry
19c/45t #6822329c45t87.0[82–89]0.9931%40%340.0605/2
29c/49t #5055599c49t86.6[80–88]1.1932%33%280.0575/3
38c/42t #584448c42t86.3[81–89]0.6527%37%390.0635/2
48c/40t #6071268c40t85.7[80–88]0.9130%37%400.0625/2
59c/43t #6467219c43t84.7[79–86]1.1231%35%300.0775/2
68c/47t #3798738c47t84.3[79–86]0.8637%34%310.0835/2
78c/39t #4208138c39t83.5[78–85]0.9734%31%340.0844/2
88c/41t #1620698c41t82.4[77–85]1.0331%37%280.0785/2
99c/46t #2440699c46t81.2[76–83]0.9738%32%300.0934/2
109c/47t #4168159c47t79.7[73–82]1.1239%28%290.1025/2
—Standard Risk World6c42t76.20.8547%21%320.1836/2

The boards (physical layouts)

Each layout is one *possible* drawing the pipeline invents for the abstract graph — a mainland + islands, land/bridge/sea attack routes, placeholder mountain/river barriers. The graph (balance) is fixed; the picture is a starting point to refine in the editor.

#1 · 9c/45t #682232 ★
